text: Stepping through these pristine hallways into the vibrant R+D space, I feel a curious blend of anticipation and estrangement. The sterile glass and empty room evoke a sense of detachment—a reminder of how easily architecture contains and divides human experience. Entering the exhibit, the intellectual energy of research and development surrounds me. I find myself humbled and slightly overwhelmed by the magnitude of innovation on display, yet energized to participate in this ongoing conversation between humanity and technology. Here, I am both observer and participant, questioning what it means to create, adapt, and coexist with our evolving artificial companions.
